edges.evidence_quote: The Canadian Hells Angels have partnered the Medellín Cartel, the Rizzuto crime family, the Sinaloa Cartel and the West End Gang in criminal operations.
edges.context: Hells Angels partnered with Medellín Cartel in criminal operations.

edges.evidence_quote: Additionally, the club has also formed alliances with various street gangs, including the Independent Soldiers, the Red Scorpions, the United Nations and the White Boy Posse, and smaller motorcycle gangs, such as Bacchus, the Gate Keepers and the Red Devils.
edges.context: Hells Angels formed alliances with Independent Soldiers.

edges.evidence_quote: On 5 December 1977, the first Canadian chapter was founded in Montreal when a club called the Popeyes led by Yves Buteau were 'patched over'.
edges.context: Popeyes patched over to become Hells Angels chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: In Western Canada, in 1983 a Vancouver club known as Satan's Angels were patched over to form the first BC chapter.
edges.context: Satan's Angels patched over to become Hells Angels chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: In December 1984, the 13th Tribe biker club in Halifax, Nova Scotia led by David 'Wolf' Carroll 'patched over' to become the first Hells Angels chapter in Atlantic Canada.
edges.context: 13th Tribe patched over to become Hells Angels chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws and several affiliated independent clubs such as Satan's Choice and Para-Dice Riders were able to keep the Angels from assuming a dominant position in Ontario, Canada's most populous province until 2000.
edges.context: Outlaws opposed Hells Angels' dominance in Ontario until 2000.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws and several affiliated independent clubs such as Satan's Choice and Para-Dice Riders were able to keep the Angels from assuming a dominant position in Ontario, Canada's most populous province until 2000.
edges.context: Satan's Choice opposed Hells Angels' dominance in Ontario.

edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws and several affiliated independent clubs such as Satan's Choice and Para-Dice Riders were able to keep the Angels from assuming a dominant position in Ontario, Canada's most populous province until 2000.
edges.context: Para-Dice Riders opposed Hells Angels' dominance in Ontario.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1997 the Grim Reapers club of Calgary, Alberta were patched over
edges.context: Grim Reapers patched over to become Hells Angels chapters.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2000, Los Bravos were 'patched over,' becoming a full-fledged HAMC chapter.
edges.context: Los Bravos patched over to become Hells Angels chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: in 1998, the Rebels of Saskatoon, Saskatchewan joined.
edges.context: Rebels joined Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2006, the Bandidos, the only possible rivals to the Hells Angels in Canada, self-destructed with the Shedden massacre, leaving the Angels as the only national outlaw biker club in Canada.
edges.context: Bandidos were rivals to Hells Angels until their self-destruction in 2006.

edges.evidence_quote: In August 1996, three men were shot dead inside a home in West Kildonan, Winnipeg as part of a feud over control of drug and prostitution rackets between members of the Manitoba Warriors and associates of the Hells Angels.
edges.context: Feud between Manitoba Warriors and Hells Angels associates over drug and prostitution rackets.

edges.evidence_quote: Project Divide culminated with 26 arrests... after the year-long investigation. The investigation and arrests targeted alleged drug-trafficking and related activities of the Zig Zag Crew – a puppet club of the Hells Angels Winnipeg chapter.
edges.context: Zig Zag Crew is a puppet club of Hells Angels Winnipeg chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: On 17 February 1978, Yves "Apache" Trudeau, the Hells Angels leading assassin, killed an Outlaw outside of a Montreal bar. In the ensuring biker war between the two gangs, Trudeau confirmed his reputation as a "psychopathic killer" as he killed 18 out of the 23 Outlaws slain during the conflict.
edges.context: Hells Angels and Outlaws fought a biker war from 1978 to 1984, with Hells Angels killing many Outlaws.

edges.evidence_quote: The Quebec Biker war between the Hells Angels and the Rock Machine began in 1994 and continued until late 2002 and claimed more than 162 lives, including innocent bystanders.
edges.context: Hells Angels and Rock Machine fought a violent biker war in Quebec from 1994 to 2002.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2023, the Quebec City chapter of the Hells Angels was reported to be engaged in a turf war with the Blood Family Mafia.
edges.context: Hells Angels Quebec City chapter and Blood Family Mafia were in a turf war in 2023.

edges.evidence_quote: In September 1998, the Rebels "patched over" to join the Hells Angels.
edges.context: The Rebels motorcycle gang patched over to become part of Hells Angels in 1998.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2000, the Hells Angels forced the disbanding of the Apollos biker gang in Regina.
edges.context: Hells Angels forced the Apollos biker gang to disband in 2000.

edges.evidence_quote: Every Quebec region had its own puppet club: the Rockers Motor Club in Montreal, the Rowdy Ones in Sorel, the Evil Ones in Drummondville, the Satan's Guard in the Saguenay region, and the Jokers in St-Jean
edges.context: Rockers Motor Club is a puppet club of Hells Angels in Montreal.
